Additive-only change verified against 5 fixed videos, ingested BEFORE and AFTER the code change, comparing every existing serialized field (all of save_index()'s manifest dict except the new scene_spans key, plus every CLIP embedding array byte-for-byte).
method: manifest_like() in scripts/partc_capture_ingest_snapshot.py reproduces save_index()'s exact manifest dict construction in-memory (frames, graph_edges, index_action_score, stats, frames_processed, peak_count, skipped_frames_ratio, storage_reduction_factor, config_snapshot -- everything except the new scene_spans key), hashed with sha256(json.dumps(manifest, sort_keys=True)); embeddings hashed separately as raw float32 bytes.
result: ALL_MATCH = true -- every existing field byte-identical on all 5 videos, before vs after.

task3_finding_zero_decode_vs_redecode.answer: scene_spans recomputation does NOT require a full video re-decode.
task3_finding_zero_decode_vs_redecode.why: charon_v.compute_valley_scene_boundaries() is a pure function of (all_frame_energies, iframe_indices, fps) -- the packet_curve tuple already extracted at ingest time via lightweight packet-size demux (its own docstring: 'Zero-decode scene boundaries from the packet-size curve'). It was already being called at ingest.py:282 before this change to assign integer scene_id -- persisting the (start,end) values it already returns costs nothing extra computationally, just an additional dict built from data already in scope.
task3_finding_zero_decode_vs_redecode.caveat_on_backfilling_ALREADY-CACHED_ind: packet_curve itself (all_frame_energies/iframe_indices/fps) is NOT persisted in the saved .npz index format (FrameRecord deliberately drops per-video-transient fields to keep the index small). So while a FRESH ingest() call gets scene_spans for free, an ALREADY-cached index from Families 1-4's tuning runs (built before this change) cannot be backfilled without re-running ingest() from the video file -- the packet curve has to be re-extracted (still zero-decode/lightweight, not a full pixel redecode, but not a pure in-memory backfill of the existing .npz either). Family 4's tuning indexes are re-ingested for the Part 3c comparison rather than backfilled in place, consistent with task 5's expectation that Method C costs more than Methods A/B.

verdict: PARITY_CONFIRMED -- Method C's ingest-side change is additive-only, all existing fields/behavior unchanged, safe to build Method C's scoring function on top of.
